title: Heaps (Priority Queues)
analogy: A heap is like an emergency room triage system — the most urgent patient (highest priority) always gets treated first, regardless of arrival order.

overview.summary: A Heap is a complete binary tree where every parent node satisfies the heap property (min or max), enabling efficient extraction of the extreme element.
learningObjectives: Implement heapify, push, and pop operations.
Apply heaps to top-K, median, and scheduling problems.
overview.interviewRelevance: Heaps are essential for top-K elements, merge-K sorted lists, and median-from-stream problems.

paragraphs: Operating systems use priority queues (heaps) to schedule processes — higher-priority tasks get CPU time first.
Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm uses a min-heap to always process the closest unvisited node.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: A heap gives you the min or max in O(1) and lets you insert/remove in O(log N) — the perfect 'what's most important right now?' structure.

definition.technicalDefinition: A heap is a complete binary tree stored as an array where the parent at index i has children at 2i+1 and 2i+2. In a min-heap, every parent ≤ its children; in a max-heap, every parent ≥ its children.

vocabulary.definition: Root is the smallest element. Parent ≤ children.
Root is the largest element. Parent ≥ children.
Restoring the heap property after an insertion or deletion.
All levels are fully filled except possibly the last, which is filled left to right.

keyProperties: Get min/max: O(1). Insert: O(log N). Delete min/max: O(log N).
Stored as a flat array — no pointer overhead.
Building a heap from N elements takes O(N), not O(N log N).

operations.name: Push (Insert)
operations.description: Add element at the end of the array, then bubble up to restore heap property.
operations.visualNote: New element appears at bottom-right, then floats upward until heap property is restored.

operations.name: Pop (Extract Min/Max)
operations.description: Remove the root, move the last element to root, then bubble down.
operations.visualNote: Root vanishes, last leaf jumps to root, then sinks down to correct position.

practice.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
practice.keyInsight: Min-heap of size K — after processing all elements, the root is the Kth largest.

practice.title: Top K Frequent Elements
practice.keyInsight: Count frequencies, then use a min-heap of size K.

practice.title: Merge K Sorted Lists
practice.keyInsight: Min-heap of K list heads; always pop the smallest and push its next.

practice.title: Find Median from Data Stream
practice.keyInsight: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half.

practice.title: Last Stone Weight
practice.keyInsight: Max-heap to always smash the two heaviest stones.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the time complexity of extracting the minimum from a min-heap?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Removing the root takes O(1), but restoring the heap property via bubble-down takes O(log N).

conceptQuiz.question: How is a heap typically stored in memory?
conceptQuiz.explanation: A heap is a complete binary tree that maps perfectly to an array using index arithmetic.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a stream of integers, efficiently find the Kth largest element at any point.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: A min-heap of size K maintains the K largest elements, with the root being the Kth largest.
